h1: 5 Design Tips for Injection-Molded Parts With Complex Features

headings.text: 5 Design Tips for Injection-Molded Parts With Complex Features
1. Take advantage of sliding shutoffs for clips and snap fits
2. Choose the right material for living hinges
3. Keep an eye on wall thickness
4. Add draft and reduce the height of tall features
5. Be strategic about text and logos
Start refining your injection molding design today
Tackling Football Head Injuries With Manufacturing Innovation
What is Geometric Dimensioning and Tolerancing (GD&T)?
Manufacturing a More Sustainable Future for a Post-Pandemic World

meta_description: While there are many viable manufacturing methods for producing parts with complex geometries, injection molding is among the most common.

sections.heading: 5 Design Tips for Injection-Molded Parts With Complex Features
sections.text: 5 Design Tips for Injection-Molded Parts With Complex Features Published on February 18, 2021 Originally published on fastradius.com on February 18, 202 1 Design for manufacturability (DFM) is the general practice of designing parts so that they are also efficient to produce. While specific best practices vary by manufacturing technology, the ultimate goal of DFM in general is to optimize part design so as to minimize the manufacturing costs — without sacrificing on performance or function. DFM also helps you identify potential issues or defects early and avoid disruptive re-designs down the line, which is why assessing possible manufacturing methods is crucial during the initial design and...
